title: DigitalOcean Spaces secret keys should not be disclosed
description: DigitalOcean Spaces uses access keys and secret keys for authentication to their S3-compatible object storage service. When these credentials are hardcoded in source code, configuration files, or version control systems, they create serious security vulnerabilities. Exposed credentials can allow unauthorized users to access, modify, or delete data in your DigitalOcean Spaces containers, potentially leading to data breaches, service disruption, and unexpected charges. Trust boundaries are violated when secrets are exposed in uncontrolled environments where unintended parties can access them.
recommendation: Remove hardcoded DigitalOcean Spaces credentials from source code. Use environment variables, secure configuration management, or secret management services instead. Rotate any exposed keys immediately. Consider using DigitalOcean's App Platform or Kubernetes secrets for secure credential management in production environments.
impact: Critical security risk - unauthorized access to DigitalOcean Spaces storage, potential data breach, service disruption, and unexpected charges

compliant.description: Reading access key from environment variable

compliant.code:

spaces_secret = "your-secret-key-here"
